message:
fix(restore_process): treat empty cwd as missing and quote empty shell_escape output

Two small but related holes in restore_process.rs surfaced when a PaneState
serialized with cwd = Some(String::new()) reached the launcher — both would
render launch commands that are silently wrong rather than erroring out.

## 1. ProcessLauncher::plan treats empty cwd as "present"

Before: .cwd.as_deref().map(normalize_cwd).unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("/"))
only fell back to "/" when cwd was None. An empty String was "present" and
normalize_cwd returned an empty PathBuf — the launch command became
  cd  && claude --resume
(note the double space) which is a syntax error in bash.

After: add .filter(|cwd| !cwd.as_os_str().is_empty()) between map and
unwrap_or_else so empty-string cwd takes the same root-fallback path as
missing cwd. Identical behavior, just closes the is_empty() gap.

## 2. shell_escape("") emitted a bare token instead of ''

Before: if the path was the empty string, neither the whitespace/metachar
branch nor the default branch wrapped it in quotes. The function returned
"" literally — and splicing "" into "cd {} && ..." produced a shell command
where the cd argument effectively vanished (cd with no arg = cd $HOME,
which is NOT what the planner intended given PathBuf::from("/") is the
fallback).

After: early-return "''" for empty input. This is the canonical POSIX way
to pass "the empty string" as a single token and is a superset-safe fix —
all existing fixtures still quote to the same strings, only the empty case
changed.

## Tests (new, inside the existing tests module)

- shell_escape_empty_path_is_quoted: pin the '' output for empty input.
- shell_escape_roundtrips_as_single_shell_token: property test across 8
  fixtures including empty string, paths with spaces, $VAR, apostrophes,
  double quotes, parens, tilde, and plain paths — each roundtrips through
  shell_words::split(&format!("cd {escaped}")) and recovers the original
  token unchanged. This is the real contract: shell_escape MUST produce
  output that a POSIX shell parser (or shell_words's model thereof) parses
  back to exactly one token equal to the input.
- plan_empty_cwd_matches_missing_cwd_root_fallback_for_shells: pin that an
  empty-cwd PaneState planning a shell launch yields LaunchShell {
  shell: "bash", cwd: PathBuf::from("/") } — same path as a None cwd.
- plan_empty_cwd_matches_missing_cwd_root_fallback_for_agents: pin that
  the agent-launch path (claude_code with cd {cwd} && claude --resume)
  renders "cd / && claude --resume" when cwd was Some("") — no bare cd,
  no empty-argument cd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
